code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test07-NOV-2018Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, suggesting possible undernutrition or overtreatment of diabetes. Elevated creatinine indicates impaired renal function. Urine findings (proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, abnormal color/appearance) further support renal impairment and possible dehydration or metabolic disturbance. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend clinical correlation for renal and metabolic status, and review of diabetes management.
